_about: Sample threat models for ThreatGuard's offline (rules-only, no API key) 'New Threat Model' flow. Thirteen example features covering classic web/API systems and LLM/agentic systems: paste `system_text` into the 'Describe in text' tab, or `structured_input` into the 'Structured' tab. Optionally group them under a release to exercise the Release -> Feature -> Threat Model hierarchy.

_usage.text_samples: New Threat Model -> 'Describe in text' -> paste `system_text` -> Create & analyze. API: POST /api/extract-from-text {"text": ...} then POST /api/threat-models.
_usage.structured_samples: New Threat Model -> 'Structured' -> paste `structured_input` -> Create & analyze. API: POST /api/extract-structured {"text": ...}. Grammar: 'Name : type' per line for components and 'A -> B : protocol, auth, encrypted|plaintext' for flows ('#'/blank lines ignored). Components and flows may carry optional security attributes in brackets, e.g. 'Support Agent : ai_agent [ingests_untrusted_content, tool_access=exec, human_in_the_loop=no]' (a bare flag means yes) — these drive evidenced findings, including the OWASP-LLM / agentic threats.
_usage.statuses: draft | in_review | in_sprint | released | cancelled; target_date is optional (YYYY-MM-DD).

release.name: 2026 Platform Hardening
release.description: Optional parent release grouping the sample features.

features.name: User Authentication & SSO
features.description: Threat focus: credential stuffing, token theft, session fixation, weak MFA.
features.system_text: Customers sign in to a React web app with email and password or via Google and Okta single sign-on. The web app calls a REST API that validates credentials against an authentication service and issues JWT access tokens with refresh-token rotation. Sessions and rate-limit counters are stored in a Redis cache, and user profiles live in a PostgreSQL database.

features.name: Payment Checkout Flow
features.description: Threat focus: PCI scope, amount tampering, payment-intent replay, webhook spoofing.
features.system_text: Shoppers check out from a web storefront that sends orders to a checkout API behind an API gateway. The checkout service tokenizes card details through the Stripe payment API and never stores card numbers. Orders are written to a PostgreSQL database, and payment webhooks from Stripe are received by a worker that updates order status.

features.name: Admin Console & RBAC
features.description: Threat focus: privilege escalation, IDOR, missing authorization, audit-log tampering.
features.system_text: Internal staff use an admin console, reachable only through a bastion host, to manage refunds and user roles. The console authenticates via SSO against an identity provider, enforces role-based access, reads and writes the accounts PostgreSQL database, and records every action to an append-only audit log.

features.name: Notification Service
features.description: Threat focus: template/HTML injection, notification spoofing, PII leakage, enumeration.
features.system_text: A notification service fans out transactional messages: emails through SendGrid, text messages through an SMS gateway, and mobile push notifications. Application services publish events to a Kafka queue, a worker renders templates, and a secrets manager holds the third-party API keys.

features.name: File Upload & Media Storage
features.description: Threat focus: unrestricted upload, path traversal, content-type confusion, malware hosting.
features.system_text: Users upload profile images and documents from a web app. Files are sent with pre-signed URLs directly to an S3 object store, scanned by a worker for malware, and served back through a CDN. File metadata is kept in a PostgreSQL database.

features.name: Public REST API & Webhooks
features.description: Threat focus: broken object-level authz, rate-limit bypass, SSRF via webhook URLs, key leakage.
features.structured_input:
Client : external_entity
API Gateway : api_gateway
REST API : api
Auth Service : auth_service
Postgres : database
Webhook Dispatcher : worker

Client -> API Gateway : https, api_key, encrypted
API Gateway -> REST API : https, jwt, encrypted
REST API -> Auth Service : https, oauth, encrypted
REST API -> Postgres : sql, password, encrypted
REST API -> Webhook Dispatcher : tcp, none, plaintext
Webhook Dispatcher -> Client : https, none, encrypted

features.name: Mobile App Backend
features.description: Threat focus: insecure deep links, device-token replay, weak transport validation, root/jailbreak abuse.
features.structured_input:
Mobile App : mobile_app
API Gateway : api_gateway
Identity Provider : identity_provider
Profile Service : service
Users DB : database
Push Service : notification_service

Mobile App -> API Gateway : https, jwt, encrypted
API Gateway -> Identity Provider : https, oauth, encrypted
API Gateway -> Profile Service : https, mtls, encrypted
Profile Service -> Users DB : sql, password, encrypted
Profile Service -> Push Service : https, api_key, encrypted

features.name: Data Export & Reporting
features.description: Threat focus: CSV formula injection, over-broad exposure, insecure temp storage, cross-tenant leakage.
features.structured_input:
Analyst : user
Reporting UI : webapp
Export Service : service
Object Store : object_storage
Data Warehouse : data_warehouse
Scheduler : scheduler
Export Worker : worker

Analyst -> Reporting UI : https, sso, encrypted
Reporting UI -> Export Service : https, jwt, encrypted
Scheduler -> Export Worker : https, none, encrypted
Export Worker -> Data Warehouse : sql, password, encrypted
Export Worker -> Object Store : https, iam, encrypted
Reporting UI -> Object Store : https, iam, encrypted

features.name: Third-party OAuth Integrations
features.description: Threat focus: token scope creep, secret storage, connect-flow CSRF, confused deputy.
features.structured_input:
User : user
Web App : webapp
Integration Service : service
Secrets Manager : secrets_manager
GitHub : external_entity
Slack : external_entity
Token DB : database

User -> Web App : https, session, encrypted
Web App -> Integration Service : https, jwt, encrypted
Integration Service -> GitHub : https, oauth, encrypted
Integration Service -> Slack : https, oauth, encrypted
Integration Service -> Secrets Manager : https, iam, encrypted

features.name: Search & Indexing Service
features.description: Threat focus: query injection, cross-tenant index access, expensive-query DoS, stale ACLs.
features.structured_input:
User : user
Web App : webapp
Search API : api
Search Cluster : search_service
Indexer : worker
Primary DB : database
Events Queue : queue

User -> Web App : https, session, encrypted
Web App -> Search API : https, jwt, encrypted
Search API -> Search Cluster : https, basic, encrypted
Primary DB -> Events Queue : tcp, none, plaintext
Events Queue -> Indexer : tcp, none, plaintext
Indexer -> Search Cluster : https, basic, encrypted

features.name: AI Support Copilot (agentic, RAG + tools)
features.description: Threat focus: prompt injection (direct + indirect via RAG), excessive agency, improper output handling, cross-user memory leakage, PII exposure. OWASP LLM Top 10 / Agentic.
features.system_text: An AI customer-support copilot. Customers chat through a public web app behind an API gateway; an orchestrator plans and calls a support agent. The agent reads untrusted customer messages and user-uploaded knowledge-base articles, retrieves answers from a vector store through a RAG retriever, calls a ticketing tool and an internal MCP server, keeps short-term session memory, and reads and writes a PostgreSQL CRM that holds customer PII. An output guardrail checks responses before they reach the user, but the agent has no prompt-injection defenses and its output is not validated before it drives tool calls.

features.name: Autonomous Agent with Tools (structured)
features.description: Threat focus: excessive agency, unsandboxed tool execution, unauthorized tool calls, prompt injection, insecure output handling. Shows the [attr=value] structured syntax.
features.structured_input:
# AI customer-support copilot (single agent). Attributes in [ ] drive the agentic findings.
Customer : user
API Gateway : api_gateway [internet_facing, authenticates_users=yes, enforces_authorization=yes, rate_limited=yes]
Support Agent : ai_agent [ingests_untrusted_content, prompt_injection_defense=no, autonomy_level=act_with_approval, tool_access=write, human_in_the_loop=yes, output_validated=no]
LLM Gateway : llm [ingests_untrusted_content, output_validated=no]
Knowledge Base : retriever [content_source_trust=user_uploaded]
Session Memory : agent_memory [memory_scope=cross_user]
Ticketing Tool : llm_tool [tool_access=exec, sandboxed=no]
CRM Database : database [handles_pii, encrypted_at_rest=yes, enforces_authorization=yes]

Customer -> API Gateway : HTTPS, session, encrypted
API Gateway -> Support Agent : HTTPS, bearer, encrypted [authorization=rbac]
Support Agent -> LLM Gateway : HTTPS, api_key, encrypted
Support Agent -> Knowledge Base : HTTPS, bearer, encrypted
Support Agent -> Session Memory : TCP, credentials, encrypted
Support Agent -> Ticketing Tool : HTTPS, api_key, encrypted [authorization=none]
Support Agent -> CRM Database : TCP, credentials, encrypted

features.name: Multi-Agent Automation Swarm (structured)
features.description: Threat focus: agent-to-agent trust & delegation (confused deputy), cascading prompt injection via a shared blackboard, cross-agent/cross-tenant memory poisoning, unbounded agent spawning, excessive agency across workers.
features.structured_input:
# Multi-Agent Research & Automation Swarm — reference agentic threat model
# A supervisor agent that plans and delegates to specialized worker agents,
# a shared blackboard/memory, and tools. Demonstrates agent-to-agent trust,
# cascading prompt injection, and delegation/confused-deputy threats.

User              : user
API Gateway       : api_gateway [internet_facing, authenticates_users=yes, enforces_authorization=yes, rate_limited=yes]
Supervisor Agent  : agent_orchestrator [autonomy_level=autonomous, human_in_the_loop=no, can_spawn_agents=yes, output_validated=no]
Research Agent    : ai_agent [ingests_untrusted_content, prompt_injection_defense=no, tool_access=read, output_validated=no, autonomy_level=autonomous, human_in_the_loop=no]
Coder Agent       : ai_agent [tool_access=exec, sandboxed=no, output_validated=no, autonomy_level=autonomous, human_in_the_loop=no]
Action Agent      : ai_agent [tool_access=write, autonomy_level=autonomous, human_in_the_loop=no, output_validated=no]
LLM Gateway       : llm [ingests_untrusted_content, output_validated=no]
Shared Blackboard : agent_memory [memory_scope=cross_tenant]
Web Retriever     : retriever [content_source_trust=web_scraped, ingests_untrusted_content]
Code Sandbox      : llm_tool [tool_access=exec, sandboxed=yes]
External APIs     : mcp_server [tool_access=write, sandboxed=no]
Vector Store      : vector_db [memory_scope=cross_tenant]
Audit Log         : datastore [logs_security_events=yes]

User -> API Gateway              : HTTPS, session, encrypted
API Gateway -> Supervisor Agent  : HTTPS, bearer, encrypted [authorization=rbac]
Supervisor Agent -> Research Agent : HTTPS, bearer, encrypted [authorization=none]
Supervisor Agent -> Coder Agent    : HTTPS, bearer, encrypted [authorization=none]
Supervisor Agent -> Action Agent   : HTTPS, bearer, encrypted [authorization=none]
Research Agent -> Web Retriever    : HTTPS, api_key, encrypted
Research Agent -> Shared Blackboard: TCP, credentials, encrypted
Coder Agent -> Shared Blackboard   : TCP, credentials, encrypted
Action Agent -> Shared Blackboard  : TCP, credentials, encrypted
Coder Agent -> Code Sandbox        : HTTPS, api_key, encrypted [authorization=none]
Action Agent -> External APIs      : HTTPS, api_key, encrypted [authorization=none]
Research Agent -> LLM Gateway      : HTTPS, api_key, encrypted
Coder Agent -> LLM Gateway         : HTTPS, api_key, encrypted
Action Agent -> LLM Gateway        : HTTPS, api_key, encrypted
Web Retriever -> Vector Store      : TCP, credentials, encrypted
Supervisor Agent -> Audit Log      : HTTPS, bearer, encrypted
